Good — this is a strong structure. Using 0, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5… gives you natural progression inside a grade without needing separate systems.

Here’s a compact, copy-ready list following that model:

CROSSMATH EDUCATION LEVEL RULES (DECIMAL PROGRESSION)

LEVEL 0

numbers: 0–10
operators: + only
addition: operands ≤ 10, result ≤ 10
equations: 1 unknown
visible: 85–95%
options: 3
goal: addition within 10

LEVEL 1

numbers: 0–10
operators: +
addition: operands ≤ 10, result ≤ 10 (allow carry)
equations: 1 unknown
visible: 75–85%
options: 3
goal: addition fluency

LEVEL 1.5

numbers: 0–10
operators: +, -
addition: operands ≤ 10
subtraction: left ≤ 10, right ≤ left (no negatives)
equations: 1 unknown
visible: 70–80%
options: 3–4
goal: intro subtraction

LEVEL 2

numbers: 0–20
operators: +, -
addition: operands ≤ 20
subtraction: left ≤ 20, right ≤ left
equations: 1–2 unknowns
visible: 65–75%
options: 3–4
goal: add/sub within 20

LEVEL 2.5

numbers: 0–20
operators: +, -
addition: operands ≤ 20
subtraction: left ≤ 20, right ≤ left
equations: 1–2 unknowns
visible: 60–70%
options: 4
goal: inverse reasoning

LEVEL 3

numbers: 0–50
operators: +, -, *
addition/subtraction: ≤ 50
multiplication:
one operand ≤ 10
second operand ≤ 5
result ≤ 50
equations: 1–2 unknowns
visible: 55–65%
options: 4
goal: intro multiplication

LEVEL 3.5

numbers: 0–50
operators: +, -, *
addition/subtraction: ≤ 50
multiplication:
both operands ≤ 10
result ≤ 100 (prefer ≤ 50 early)
equations: 2 unknowns allowed
visible: 50–60%
options: 4
goal: multiplication fluency

LEVEL 4

numbers: 0–100
operators: +, -, *, /
addition/subtraction: ≤ 100
multiplication: operands ≤ 10 (extend gradually)
division:
exact only
divisor ≤ 10
result ≤ 10–20
equations: 2–3 unknowns
visible: 45–55%
options: 4
goal: mixed arithmetic

LEVEL 4.5

numbers: 0–100
operators: +, -, *, /
addition/subtraction: ≤ 100
multiplication: operands ≤ 12
division:
exact only
divisor ≤ 12
result ≤ 12
equations: multi-step
visible: 40–50%
options: 4–5
goal: structured reasoning

LEVEL 5

numbers: 0–200
operators: +, -, *, /, avg, min, max
addition/subtraction: ≤ 200
multiplication: operands ≤ 15
division: exact, divisor ≤ 15
avg: (a+b)/2 integer only
min/max: unrestricted within bounds
equations: multi-unknown
visible: 35–45%
options: 4–5
goal: logical reasoning

LEVEL 5.5

numbers: 0–200
operators: +, -, *, /, avg, min, max
multiplication: operands ≤ 20
division: divisor ≤ 20
avg: integer only
equations: complex intersections
visible: 30–40%
options: 5
goal: multi-step deduction

LEVEL 6+

numbers: 0–500
operators: all + optional ^, root
multiplication: operands ≤ 25
division: divisor ≤ 25
exponent:
base ≤ 10
exponent ≤ 3
root:
degree 2–3
perfect powers only
equations: chain reasoning
visible: 20–35%
options: 5
goal: advanced reasoning

GLOBAL RULES

always keep ≥1 anchor per equation
early levels: operators always visible
hide non-intersection cells first
intersections become critical after level 3
ensure single correct answer
selection options include:
correct
near mistake
misconception

KEY INSIGHT

difficulty is controlled per operator, not just max number
multiplication/division must scale independently
early multiplication = small factors, not small results

PROGRESSION LOGIC

increase difficulty by:
number range
operator complexity
hidden %
intersection dependency

This structure is strong because:

👉 .0 = start of grade (learning)
👉 .5 = end of grade (mastery + complexity)
